recent_lessons: Hartenstein REB T6 missed (actual 5, one below threshold) in a game where he played only 16 minutes — the blowout game script was the direct driver; his 31 minutes in G5 vs. 16 minutes in G6 is a stark blowout compression example; the Wemby interior competition penalty was correctly noted (-5pp) but the pick still cleared at 72%, suggesting the blowout_risk signal for a non-anchor big on the road in a team that might bench players early needed to be weighted more aggressively.

Both Jalen Williams picks (PTS T10 and AST T2) missed because he played only 10 minutes with a confirmed hamstring injury management restriction — the analyst correctly identified the binary risk and applied a conservative 70% confidence on both, and the QUES status was flagged; however the pick was still emitted rather than skipped, and the actual outcome (10 minutes, 1 PTS, 1 AST) was a direct realization of the binary tail risk the analyst described; when a player's previous-series injury produced a 7-minute game and the current designation is QUES with documented hamstring management, emitting at 70% rather than skipping requires iron-floor-level structural support that was absent here.

Vassell 3PM T2 MISS (actual 1): The WCF series 6/6 at T2 was the primary basis for the pick, and the iron_floor override by VOLATILE was correctly identified and applied. However, the within-series perfect record at T2 should be weighted more cautiously when the iron_floor is explicitly overridden — the safety net that creates the structural floor (iron_floor) was removed, leaving only the VOLATILE-penalized hit rate (72%, at the floor) and the within-series streak. A 72% confidence at a minimum floor for a 3PM prop with VOLATILE tag and iron_floor overridden is a structurally fragile combination, particularly in a road G7 against OKC's elite defense (opp_defense_rating logged as 'unknown' but OKC is the #1 seed with elite defensive personnel). The post-game news confirms a cold shooting night with no external factor — this is pure variance at T2 on a 72% pick, but the pick was at its absolute minimum acceptable confidence level with the primary structural support (iron_floor) removed.

Hartenstein REB T6 MISS (actual 5): The post-game news confirms Hartenstein played only 21 minutes, well below his recent workload of 25-30 minutes. The pick's reasoning cited 'SAS backup bigs all OUT' as a favorable rebounding environment, but the actual limiting factor was Hartenstein's own playing time — 21 minutes is insufficient to reliably generate 6+ rebounds even for an elite rebounder. The G6 miss (2 REB) triggered the slump-persistent -5pp penalty, bringing confidence to exactly 72% (the floor). The 25th-pct gate passed (3rd-lowest L10 was 7, above the T6 pick value of 6), but the WCF series variance (5,15,7,8,13,2 — a 2 and a 5 in back-to-back games entering G7) should have been a stronger signal that Hartenstein's rebounding output in this series is highly volatile game-to-game, not merely trend=stable. The minutes compression in a competitive CLOSE game (margin 8) without a blowout explanation represents genuine role volatility for a backup center.

Hartenstein AST T2 MISS (actual 0): The WCF series within-series record was 6/6 at T2 (3,4,3,4,3,2), with the G6 value being exactly 2 — the minimum hit. The pick reasoning correctly applied VOLATILE -3% and arrived at 75% (75% before amendment, 72% target). The actual outcome of 0 AST in only 21 minutes is partially explained by the reduced playing time identified in the Hartenstein REB analysis — 21 minutes severely limits assist opportunities for a backup center. The critical miss signal here is that the two Hartenstein props (REB T6 and AST T2) share the same root cause: an unexpected minutes compression in G7 that the system did not anticipate. When a backup center's minutes are volatile game-to-game in a playoff series (WCF minutes: varied 18-33+ across games), both rebounding AND assist props become exposed to the same minutes-floor risk simultaneously. Picking two props on the same player in the same game doubles exposure to the shared minutes-compression tail risk.

Fox 3PM T1 missed (0 actual vs pick_value 1): The trend=down signal was a genuine structural warning that the system partially acknowledged but ultimately overrode via the 9+/10 exception. Post-game news confirms Fox was battling a right ankle injury since the WCF Semifinals and went 0-for-4 from three on 3-13 overall shooting. The trend=down tag reflected this degradation. The 10/11 recent series rate used to qualify the exception was accumulated largely before the ankle injury manifested as a 3PM-specific limitation. This is a model_gap_rule miss: the ankle injury context was not formally available in the pick object's injury_status_at_check (NOT_LISTED), but the trend=down tag was present and the post-game news confirms ankle involvement. The system lacks a rule that treats trend=down on 3PM + known mid-series ankle injury as a compound skip signal rather than a recoverable exception.

Vassell PTS T10 missed by 1 (9 actual vs pick_value 10): A narrow miss-by-1 in 36 minutes of normal play with no injury or external factor. The hit_rate_display was 9/10 and the vs_tough degradation from 90%→73% was the primary structural warning. The actual result (9 pts) landed exactly on the wrong side of the threshold with no unusual game-script factor. This is a variance miss — the tier was at the edge of viability with documented vs_tough degradation, and the player simply had an ordinary off-scoring night.

Brunson AST T6 missed badly (2 actual vs pick_value 6): The pick object shows walked_tier=6 but the reasoning_override indicates the analyst intended T4 (pick_value=4 in the override note). The actual pick as graded has pick_value=6 and walked_tier=6, so this is graded as a T6 miss (actual 2 vs 6). The tier_walk correctly identified T6 as borderline (70% exactly at ceiling rule minimum) and the VOLATILE -8% primary playmaker penalty dropped confidence to 62% below the AST floor — at which point T4 was the correct selection. The pick ran at T6 with pick_value=6 despite the analysis explicitly concluding T4 was correct. This is a selection_error: the reasoning_override note documents the analyst identified the error but the pick object was not corrected before running. Additionally, the competitive=67% at T6 was a direct red flag for a spread=4.5 competitive Finals game — Brunson's shot-heavy usage mode (30 points on 31 shots per post-game news) is a known AST suppression pattern in scoring-mode games that the system has flagged in prior series (Edwards in DEN-MIN, Brunson in ATL series G5). The 2 AST result is consistent with primary scorer isolation usage, not playmaking mode.

OG Anunoby REB T4 missed by 1 (3 actual vs pick_value 4): A narrow miss-by-1 in 31 minutes of normal play with no injury factor confirmed. The hit_rate_display was 8/10 and trend=down was present. The PHI series showed 0/2 at T6, and the pick object notes PHI series T4=1/2 (one miss below 4). Combined with trend=down, the structural evidence was mixed — ATL series was excellent (6/6) while PHI was poor. The minimum-tier T4 cap at 72% was correctly applied. This is a variance miss: the pick was at the minimum viable confidence floor with documented series volatility, and the narrow miss-by-1 is consistent with the tail risk already priced into the 72% confidence.

Castle AST T4 missed (3 actual vs pick_value 4, injury exit confirmed): The pick object has post_game_event=injury_exit confirmed. Castle exited to the locker room after getting injured (Wembanyama play involvement per post-game news) and did not return. His 3 assists were accumulated in 34 minutes before the exit. The miss is attributable to the injury exit — he was tracking normally before the exit (17 pts, 8 reb already in hand), and the AST total was limited by the reduced late-game playmaking time. This is an injury_event miss.
recent_reinforcements: SGA PTS T20 iron_floor directive validated again: SGA scored 35 in 43 minutes of a competitive G7 (OKC won by 8), clearing the threshold by 15 points on a night where every structural signal — G7 elimination, Williams OUT usage spike, LATE_RISER modifier, home court — aligned. The tier_walk correctly declined the T20→T15 walk-revert (no market at T15) and the within-series G6 sub-20 penalty (-5pp) was appropriately conservative but did not prevent a correct selection. T20 is a structural lock for SGA regardless of FG efficiency concerns — this result is another data point in a multi-hundred-game streak.

Vassell PTS T10 perfect-series consistency translated cleanly to G7: The WCF+MIN combined 12/12 T10 streak held, with Vassell posting 11 points in 33 minutes of a CLOSE game. The FG_MARGIN_THIN walk-revert penalty (-5pp in lieu of a step to T5 with no market) was correctly applied and the pick still cleared the 75% confidence threshold. The reasoning that 'population-level 57.6% FG_MARGIN_THIN hit rate is less predictive than a 12/12 individual series streak' was validated — per-player consistency data correctly outweighed the population-level warning flag.

Castle PTS T10 (walked down from T15 via FG_MARGIN_NEG) and Castle REB T4 both hit in a CLOSE G7: The FG_MARGIN_NEG step-down from T15 to T10 for Castle PTS was correctly executed — actual was 16, clearing T10 comfortably — and the WCF series 6/6 within-series data proved reliable. Castle REB T4 at the 72% cap (minimum-tier T4 with VOLATILE and slump-persistent penalties) also hit with 6 rebounds, a full 2 rebounds above the threshold. The minimum-tier cap discipline (72% ceiling when B2B validation is null) kept confidence appropriately bounded while still capturing the pick.

Fox PTS T10 hit despite ankle carry-over penalty and trend=down: The -5pp ankle carry-over penalty and -3pp VOLATILE deduction were correctly applied, bringing confidence from ~83% to 72% at T10. Fox scored 15 in 36 minutes of a CLOSE G7, clearing T10 by 5 units. The WCF series 6/6 T10 perfect within-series record was the decisive signal. The ankle concern did not manifest as a minutes restriction or scoring floor collapse in a competitive game context — the penalty calibration appears correctly sized for a NOT_LISTED ankle in a G7 elimination game.

Dylan Harper AST T2 hit at 76% confidence: H2H vs OKC 9/10 at T2 was the primary supporting signal, with WCF series 6/6 within-series at T2 providing corroboration. Harper posted 3 AST in 27 minutes, clearing T2 by 1. The VOLATILE -3pp deduction was correctly applied, and the H2H signal modifier (+4pp back from 72%) was justified given the strength of the 9/10 rate. The tier_walk correctly identified that T4 failed at 2/6 in the WCF series, making T2 the only viable selection — the walk was disciplined and the outcome confirmed it.

Wembanyama PTS T15 (82% confidence, HIT 26pts): The FG_MARGIN_THIN mandatory step-down from T20→T15 was the correct mechanism. Despite a cold shooting night (6-21 FG), Wembanyama's blowout exemption and structural scoring floor held. The playoff -3pp penalty was appropriately conservative. This validates the FG_MARGIN_THIN step-down rule as properly protective — it avoided T20 exposure on a night where SGA-level efficiency was not present, while still capturing a clean T15 result.

Fox AST T4 (83% confidence, HIT 5 AST): The tier-walk discipline of stepping past T6 (which had thin evidence in series data) down to T4 where the near-perfect 11/11 recent playoff rate lived was the right call. The VOLATILE -8% primary-playmaker penalty correctly constrained T6 without abandoning the prop entirely. The 88% vs-tough confirmation and H2H 3/3 at T6 provided appropriate secondary validation. This is a textbook example of walking to the tier with the strongest evidence base rather than the highest available tier.

Brunson 3PM T1 iron_floor (80% confidence, HIT 2 3PM): The iron_floor designation correctly protected this pick despite the road Finals context and trend=stable. The 9/10 hit rate at T1 with iron_floor exemption from the 80% non-iron_floor cap was correctly applied. This reinforces that iron_floor tags on 3PM props should be treated as structural locks at T1 regardless of opponent toughness — Brunson delivered 2 threes against SAS's elite defense.

Castle PTS T10 (75% confidence, HIT 17pts) and REB T4 (72% confidence, HIT 8 reb): Both Castle props cleared decisively despite his mid-game injury exit. The props hit prior to the exit, validating the underlying tier selection. The FG_MARGIN_NEG step-down from T15→T10 on PTS and the minimum-tier T4 exception on REB were both correctly applied — the conservative step-down logic protected against the upside-risk tiers while still capturing the floor production that materialized in Castle's active minutes.

OG Anunoby PTS T10 (78% confidence, HIT 17pts) and 3PM T1 (75% confidence, HIT 3 3PM): The vs_tough degradation penalty on PTS (-5pp informal, from 90% to 78%) was appropriately conservative given SAS's defensive rating, and the pick still cleared comfortably. The trend=down mandatory step-down T2→T1 on 3PM with the -5pp exception penalty and 80% non-iron_floor cap was correctly executed — OG's 8/8 recent playoff T1 rate proved durable even in a road Finals opener against elite defense.
recent_recommendations: AMENDMENT UPSIDE SIGNAL ON QUES PLAYERS: For the two Williams picks, the lineup_update flagged direction='unchanged' at pick time (the amendment was triggered by Williams being QUES but revised_confidence stayed at 70%). When the lineup_update.triggered_by includes the same player as the pick subject and that player is QUES, the amendment agent should force a re-evaluation of the skip threshold rather than confirming 'unchanged' — the amendment correctly identified the risk but did not translate it into a skip recommendation; this feedback loop should be tightened so QUES self-referential triggers automatically escalate to skip review.

DUAL-PROP SAME-PLAYER MINUTES RISK RULE: When selecting two or more props on the same backup/secondary player in the same playoff game, require that the player's projected minutes (proj_min) exceed 25 AND that their L6 series minutes show no game with fewer than 20 minutes. If either condition fails, restrict to a maximum of one prop on that player (choose the highest-confidence tier). This rule directly addresses the Hartenstein REB+AST double miss, where both props were lost to the same unexpected 21-minute load — a shared single-point-of-failure that was not identified as a compound risk at selection time.

VOLATILE + IRON_FLOOR OVERRIDDEN + 72% FLOOR = CONDITIONAL SKIP FOR 3PM: When a 3PM prop carries all three of: (1) VOLATILE tag, (2) iron_floor explicitly overridden by VOLATILE, and (3) final confidence at or below 74%, treat this as a skip candidate rather than a minimum-floor emit. The Vassell 3PM miss demonstrates that the iron_floor override removes the structural safety net that justifies picking at minimum confidence levels — without iron_floor, a 72-74% 3PM pick in a road playoff game against elite defense has insufficient structural support. Require either iron_floor to be intact OR confidence above 78% before emitting a VOLATILE-tagged 3PM prop.

BACKUP CENTER G7 MINUTES VOLATILITY ADJUSTMENT: For backup centers (raw_avgs PTS < 15 and role = reserve/backup) in a G7 or elimination game, apply an additional -5pp confidence penalty to ALL counting stat props (REB, AST, PTS) unless the player has logged 28+ minutes in at least 4 of the last 6 series games. The rationale: G7 game scripts often produce unexpected role compression for backup bigs when coaches shorten rotations or when game flow demands defensive matchup changes. Hartenstein's 21-minute G7 load occurred in a CLOSE game without a blowout explanation — pure rotation compression. This penalty applies on top of existing VOLATILE and trend penalties.

WCF/SERIES WITHIN-SERIES STREAK DECAY FOR BACKUP BIGS: When a backup player (proj_min < 28) has a within-series perfect streak (e.g., 6/6 at T2) but their most recent two series games show a miss or minimum hit at the threshold (e.g., Hartenstein G5=2 REB, G6=2 AST exactly at threshold), apply a within-series recency decay: discount the 6/6 streak to an effective 4/6 for confidence calculation purposes. Back-to-back minimum hits signal the player is approaching their floor, not comfortably clearing it. This would have reduced Hartenstein's AST confidence below 72% and potentially triggered a skip.

COMPOUND SKIP RULE — trend=down + active injury context on 3PM: When a player carries trend=down on 3PM AND there is any documented mid-series injury (even NOT_LISTED at pick time) affecting a shooting-relevant body part (ankle, wrist, hand), treat the combination as a hard skip at T1 regardless of recent hit rate. Do not apply the 9+/10 exception when both signals are present simultaneously. Fox 3PM T1 demonstrates that a 10/11 recent rate can be built on pre-injury games, making the exception threshold stale. Operationalize: check for any WEB_RECAP or series diary injury reference before invoking the trend=down T1 exception.

SCORING-MODE AST SUPPRESSION RULE — primary playmaker in shot-heavy game scripts: For guards with raw_avgs AST ≥ 6.0 who are also primary scorers (raw_avgs PTS ≥ 25), add a formal 'scoring-mode override' signal: when a player's recent playoff games show PTS/AST inverse correlation (high scoring = low AST pattern visible in series diary), apply an additional -5pp penalty to any AST T6+ pick regardless of vs_tough or H2H data. Brunson's series history shows this pattern explicitly — his 30-pt G1 produced only 2 AST. The competitive=67% at T6 already flagged the risk; a formal scoring-mode suppression rule would have pushed confidence below the AST floor and triggered a skip.

WALK-DOWN EXECUTION DISCIPLINE — reasoning_override contradictions must void the pick: When the analyst's reasoning_override field explicitly states 'this pick should be at a different tier/value than walked_tier,' the pick must be suppressed and reissued at the corrected tier before running. Brunson AST ran at T6/pick_value=6 despite the analyst documenting mid-reasoning that T4 was the correct selection. Implement a validation check: if reasoning_override is present and contains corrected tier language, flag the pick as NEEDS_REISSUE and do not run it until corrected. This is a workflow integrity issue, not an analytical gap.

vs_TOUGH DEGRADATION THRESHOLD — formalize the mandatory step-down trigger: When vs_tough drops more than 15pp below overall hit rate at the selected tier, treat this as a mandatory step-down signal equivalent to FG_MARGIN_THIN — not a discretionary penalty. Vassell PTS T10 showed vs_tough=73% vs overall=90% (17pp gap) and the penalty applied was only -7pp, leaving confidence at 78% and the pick running at T10. A mandatory step-down to T7 (or skip if no market exists) when the vs_tough gap exceeds 15pp would have prevented this near-miss. This rule applies to all prop types where opp_defense is a rated input (PTS, AST) — not to REB or 3PM where opp_defense is treated as noise.

FINALS SERIES OPENER — new-opponent adjustment for trend=down props: In the first game of any new series (G1), apply an additional -3pp to any pick with trend=down regardless of prop type. The trend=down tag reflects performance trajectory against recent opponents, but a new opponent in a new series (especially one as defensively distinct as SAS vs NYK, or NYK vs SAS) introduces structural uncertainty not captured in cross-series hit rates. This would have further suppressed Fox 3PM (already borderline at 78%), OG 3PM (already at 75%), and Castle REB (already at 72%), helping calibrate G1 slate confidence more conservatively while preserving iron_floor and 9+/10 exceptions for truly structural props.
